title: People-Machinery Interaction
body: PMI failures produce the worst outcomes on site — a truck that didn't see you, an operator you assumed saw you, and the result is severe or fatal. At ADW, every shift has dozens of machine-approach moments at the loading face, ROM pad, and ramps where this chain can break.
keyPoints: The operator must have actually seen you before you move toward any machine — not assumed to have seen you, actually seen you.
Wait for the machine to park, GET on the ground, and the operator to signal before approaching. No exceptions, no shortcuts.
No vehicle parks behind operating surface mobile equipment. Full stop.
The 50 m exclusion board is the boundary — inside 50 m, the radio call, GET-down, and signal-to-approach sequence is mandatory, not optional.
Amber strobes, headlights, and clean mirrors are required on every machine in the mining area at all times. A failed light means the machine parks until the fault is raised.
apply: At the loading face and ROM pad, check whether light vehicles are sitting inside the 50 m zone without having gone through the radio-call and approach sequence — it's the most common way this rule gets quietly bent during high-tempo dig cycles.
